topic: Google Antigravity IDE Review 2026: The Agent-First IDE Worth It?

competitors.title: Google Antigravity Review 2026: Is the Agent-First IDE Worth It?
key_points: Agent-first IDE where AI agents execute code across editor, terminal, and browser surfaces
Two working modes: Editor View (VS Code-like with AI panel) vs Manager View (mission control for parallel agents)
Artifact system produces implementation plans, task lists, screenshots, and video recordings as proof of work
Multi-model support: Gemini 3 Pro, Claude Sonnet 4.5, GPT-OSS 120B — switch mid-session
Free during preview but 'generous rate limits' are opaque; AI Pro at $19.99/month bundled with Google AI Pro subscription
Community divided: 'future of coding' vs '$20 paperweight' — both camps have valid points
Subscription syncing bug where AI Pro users were incorrectly treated as free-tier users
40% faster than Cursor 2.0 on large codebase queries, 94% accuracy vs Cursor's 78% on multi-file refactoring (third-party testing)
Gemini 3 Pro scores 76.2% on SWE-bench Verified
1M token context window handles entire monorepos
Weaknesses: preview instability, agent termination errors, micro-decision fatigue, debugging weakness on complex logic

competitors.title: Google AntiGravity Review 2026: Agent-First IDE Worth Using?
key_points: Updated April 2026 with model upgrades: Claude Sonnet 4.6 and Claude Opus 4.6 (up from 4.5)
Full model roster: Gemini 3.1 Pro (High/Low), Gemini 3 Flash, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.6, GPT-OSS 120B
AgentKit 2.0 (March 2026): 16 specialized agents, 40+ domain-specific skills, 11 pre-configured commands
MCP support added in early 2026 for external service connections (GitHub, databases, APIs)
Pricing tiers: Free $0/mo, AI Pro $20/mo, AI Ultra $249.99/mo, Credits $25/2,500
'Paperweight' controversy: users report throttled high-reasoning models, opaque credit system, weekly vs 5-hour rate limit resets
Enterprise rating: 4.4/5 vs Cursor 4.7/5 — AntiGravity wins on agentic depth; Cursor wins on stability/compliance
Version 1.20.3 added AGENTS.md support alongside GEMINI.md rules files
Security warning: agents can issue aggressive shell commands like chmod -R 777 — use sandbox/VM
No compliance certifications (no SOC 2) — enterprise features listed as 'coming soon'
Built-in Chromium browser is standout feature for frontend developers and QA workflows

competitors.title: Google Antigravity: The Agent-First IDE That Wants to Replace Your Entire Workflow
key_points: 6% developer adoption within just two months of launch — fastest-growing AI dev tool on the market
4.7/5 rating on Product Hunt from 60 reviews
Self-improving knowledge base: agents save context, code snippets, patterns to project knowledge base over time
Custom 'Skills' feature: reusable instructions teaching agents team best practices
AntiGravity vs Cursor vs Copilot vs Claude Code comparison: multi-agent parallel execution unique to AntiGravity
Pricing backlash: quotas cut by as much as 97% compared to pre-January 2026 levels (The Register)
Credit system opacity is major frustration — what does one credit actually buy?
Value proposition of $20/month AI Pro is strong IF quota sufficient — includes Opus access that costs much more via API
No intermediate tier between $20 and $250 — gap felt acutely by moderate users
Browser automation compared to Cypress/Playwright for end-to-end test development potential
Enterprise team features (SSO, SLAs) listed as 'coming soon' — not yet suitable for regulated environments
Common recommendation: use for rapid prototyping/experimentation, keep Cursor/Claude Code as daily driver

stats.fact: 6% developer adoption within two months of launch
4.7/5 rating on Product Hunt from 60 reviews
Gemini 3 Pro scores 76.2% on SWE-bench Verified
40% faster than Cursor 2.0 on large codebase queries (100K+ lines)
94% accuracy on multi-file refactoring vs Cursor's 78%
Quotas cut by up to 97% compared to pre-January 2026 levels
AI Ultra tier at $249.99/month — no intermediate tier between $20 and $250
AgentKit 2.0 ships 16 specialized agents and 40+ domain-specific skills
1M token context window handles entire monorepos without truncation
Enterprise ratings: AntiGravity 4.4/5 vs Cursor 4.7/5 vs Copilot 4.3/5
53% generative AI adoption globally in three years — faster than PC or internet
88% AI adoption in tech industry organizations

Artifacts as trust mechanism: making autonomous agents auditable and reviewable
